1.author: Ahmed Ali
1.text: I CALL THE night to witness when it covers over,

1.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
1.text: By oath of the night when it covers

1.author: Daryabadi
1.text: By the night when it envelopeth,

1.author: Hilali & Khan
1.text: By the night as it envelops;

1.author: Itani
1.text: By the night as it covers.

1.author: Maududi
1.text: By the night when it enshrouds,

1.author: Mubarakpuri
1.text: By the night as it envelops.

1.author: Qarai
1.text: By the night when it envelops,

1.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
1.text: By the night, when it envelops,

1.author: Saheeh International
1.text: By the night when it covers

1.author: Sarwar
1.text: By the night when it covers the day,

1.author: Shakir
1.text: I swear by the night when it draws a veil,

1.author: Wahiduddin Khan
1.text: By the night as it veils [the earth] in darkness,

1.author: Yusuf Ali
1.text: By the Night as it conceals (the light);

2.author: Ahmed Ali
2.text: And the day when it shines in all its glory,

2.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
2.text: And by oath of the day when it shines

2.author: Daryabadi
2.text: By the day when it appeareth in glory,

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: And by the day as it appears in brightness;

2.author: Itani
2.text: And the day as it reveals.

2.author: Maududi
2.text: and by the day when it breaks in its glory,

2.author: Mubarakpuri
2.text: By the day as it appears.

2.author: Qarai
2.text: by the day when it brightens,

2.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
2.text: and by the day when it unveils.

2.author: Saheeh International
2.text: And [by] the day when it appears

2.author: Sarwar
2.text: by the day when it appears radiant,

2.author: Shakir
2.text: And the day when it shines in brightness,

2.author: Wahiduddin Khan
2.text: and by the day as it appears radiantly,

2.author: Yusuf Ali
2.text: By the Day as it appears in glory;

3.author: Ahmed Ali
3.text: And Him who created the male and female,

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: And by oath of the One Who created the male and the female.

3.author: Arberry
3.text: and That which created the male and the female,

3.author: Daryabadi
3.text: By Him Who hath created the male and the female,

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: And by Him Who created male and female;

3.author: Itani
3.text: And He who created the male and the female.

3.author: Maududi
3.text: and by Him Who created the male and the female:

3.author: Mubarakpuri
3.text: By Him Who created male and female.

3.author: Pickthall
3.text: And Him Who hath created male and female,

3.author: Qarai
3.text: by Him who created the male and the female:

3.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
3.text: And by Who created the male and female,

3.author: Saheeh International
3.text: And [by] He who created the male and female,

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: and by that (Power) which created the male and female,

3.author: Shakir
3.text: And the creating of the male and the female,

3.author: Wahiduddin Khan
3.text: and by the creation of the male and the female.

3.author: Yusuf Ali
3.text: By (the mystery of) the creation of male and female;-

4.author: Ahmed Ali
4.text: That your endeavour is for different ends.

4.author: Arberry
4.text: surely your striving is to diverse ends.

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: Certainly, your efforts and deeds are diverse (different in aims and purposes);

4.author: Mubarakpuri
4.text: Certainly, your efforts and deeds are diverse.

4.author: Pickthall
4.text: Lo! your effort is dispersed (toward divers ends).

4.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
4.text: your striving is indeed to different ends!

4.author: Shakir
4.text: Your striving is most surely (directed to) various (ends).

4.author: Wahiduddin Khan
4.text: O men, you truly strive towards the most diverse ends!

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: Verily, (the ends) ye strive for are diverse.

5.author: Ahmed Ali
5.text: Yet he who gives to others and has fear,

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: So for one who gave and practised piety

5.author: Arberry
5.text: As for him who gives and is godfearing

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: Then as for him who giveths and feareth Him.

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: As for him who gives (in charity) and keeps his duty to Allah and fears Him,

5.author: Itani
5.text: As for him who gives and is righteous.

5.author: Maududi
5.text: As for him who gave out his wealth (for Allah's sake) and abstained (from disobeying Him),

5.author: Mubarakpuri
5.text: As for him who gives and has Taqwa,

5.author: Pickthall
5.text: As for him who giveth and is dutiful (toward Allah)

5.author: Qarai
5.text: As for him who gives and is Godwary

5.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
5.text: For him that gives and fears (Allah)

5.author: Saheeh International
5.text: As for he who gives and fears Allah

5.author: Sarwar
5.text: We shall facilitate the path to bliss

5.author: Shakir
5.text: Then as for him who gives away and guards (against evil),

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: As for one who gives [to others] and fears [God],

5.author: Yusuf Ali
5.text: So he who gives (in charity) and fears (Allah),

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: And believed the best matter to be true

6.author: Arberry
6.text: and confirms the reward most fair,

6.author: Maududi
6.text: and affirmed the Truth of goodness:

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: for those who spend for the cause of God,

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: and believes in the truth of what is right,

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: And (in all sincerity) testifies to the best,-

7.author: Ahmed Ali
7.text: We shall ease the way of fortune for him.

7.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
7.text: So We will very soon provide him ease.

7.author: Arberry
7.text: We shall surely ease him to the Easing.

7.author: Daryabadi
7.text: Unto him therefore We shall indeed make easy the path to ease.

7.author: Hilali & Khan
7.text: We will make smooth for him the path of ease (goodness).

7.author: Itani
7.text: We will ease his way towards ease.

7.author: Maududi
7.text: We shall facilitate for him the Way to Bliss.

7.author: Mubarakpuri
7.text: We will make smooth for him the path of ease.

7.author: Pickthall
7.text: Surely We will ease his way unto the state of ease.

7.author: Qarai
7.text: We will surely ease him toward facility.

7.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
7.text: We shall surely ease him to the Path of Easing;

7.author: Saheeh International
7.text: We will ease him toward ease.

7.author: Sarwar
7.text: observe piety, and believe in receiving rewards from God.

7.author: Shakir
7.text: We will facilitate for him the easy end.

7.author: Wahiduddin Khan
7.text: We will pave his way to ease.

7.author: Yusuf Ali
7.text: We will indeed make smooth for him the path to Bliss.

8.author: Ahmed Ali
8.text: But he who does not give and is unconcerned,

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: And for him who hoarded wealth and remained carefree,

8.author: Arberry
8.text: But as for him who is a miser, and self-sufficient,

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: And as for him who stinteth and is heedless,

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: But he who is greedy miser and thinks himself self-sufficient.

8.author: Itani
8.text: But as for him who is stingy and complacent.

8.author: Maududi
8.text: As for him who was a miser and behaved with aversion (to Allah),

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: But he who is greedy and thinks himself self-sufficient,

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: But as for him who hoardeth and deemeth himself independent,

8.author: Qarai
8.text: But as for him, who is stingy and self-complacent,

8.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
8.text: but for him that is a miser, and sufficed,

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: But as for he who withholds and considers himself free of need

8.author: Sarwar
8.text: But for those who are niggardly, horde their wealth,

8.author: Shakir
8.text: And as for him who is niggardly and considers himself free from need (of Allah),

8.author: Wahiduddin Khan
8.text: But as for one who is miserly and unheeding,

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: But he who is a greedy miser and thinks himself self-sufficient,

9.author: Arberry
9.text: and cries lies to the reward most fair,

9.author: Hilali & Khan
9.text: And gives the lie to Al-Husna (see Verse No: 6 footnote);

9.author: Maududi
9.text: and denied the Truth of goodness:

9.author: Sarwar
9.text: and have no faith in receiving any reward (from God).

9.author: Yusuf Ali
9.text: And gives the lie to the best,-

10.author: Ahmed Ali
10.text: For him We shall ease the way of adversity,

10.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
10.text: So We will very soon provide him hardship.

10.author: Arberry
10.text: We shall surely ease him to the Hardship;

10.author: Daryabadi
10.text: Unto him therefore We shall Indeed make easy the path to hardship.

10.author: Hilali & Khan
10.text: We will make smooth for him the path for evil;

10.author: Itani
10.text: We will ease his way towards difficulty.

10.author: Maududi
10.text: We shall facilitate for him the way to Hardship,

10.author: Mubarakpuri
10.text: We will make smooth for him the path to evil.

10.author: Pickthall
10.text: Surely We will ease his way unto adversity.

10.author: Qarai
10.text: We will surely ease him toward hardship.

10.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
10.text: We shall surely ease for him the Path of Hardship (the Fire).

10.author: Saheeh International
10.text: We will ease him toward difficulty.

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: We shall facilitate the path to affliction

10.author: Shakir
10.text: We will facilitate for him the difficult end.

10.author: Wahiduddin Khan
10.text: We shall pave his way to hardship,

10.author: Yusuf Ali
10.text: We will indeed make smooth for him the path to Misery;

11.author: Ahmed Ali
11.text: And his riches will not avail him when he falls headlong (into the Abyss).

11.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
11.text: His wealth will not avail him when he falls into ruin.

11.author: Arberry
11.text: his wealth shall not avail him when he perishes.

11.author: Daryabadi
11.text: And his substance will avail him not when he perisheth.

11.author: Hilali & Khan
11.text: And what will his wealth benefit him when he goes down (in destruction).

11.author: Itani
11.text: And his money will not avail him when he plummets.

11.author: Maududi
11.text: and his wealth shall be of no avail to him when he perishes.

11.author: Mubarakpuri
11.text: And what will his wealth avail him when he goes down (in destruction)

11.author: Pickthall
11.text: His riches will not save him when he perisheth.

11.author: Qarai
11.text: His wealth will not avail him when he perishes.

11.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
11.text: When he falls (into Hell), his wealth will not help him.

11.author: Saheeh International
11.text: And what will his wealth avail him when he falls?

11.author: Sarwar
11.text: and their wealth will be of no benefit to them when they face destruction.

11.author: Shakir
11.text: And his wealth will not avail him when he perishes.

11.author: Wahiduddin Khan
11.text: nor will his wealth profit him when he falls [into the pit].

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: Nor will his wealth profit him when he falls headlong (into the Pit).

11.author: Transliteration
11.text: Wama yughnee AAanhu maluhu itha taradda

12.author: Ahmed Ali
12.text: It is indeed for Us to show the way,

12.author: Arberry
12.text: Surely upon Us rests the guidance,

12.author: Daryabadi
12.text: Verify on us is the guidance

12.author: Hilali & Khan
12.text: Truly! Ours it is (to give) guidance,

12.author: Itani
12.text: It is upon Us to guide.

12.author: Maududi
12.text: Surely it is for Us to show the Right Way,

12.author: Mubarakpuri
12.text: Truly, on Us is (to give) guidance.

12.author: Pickthall
12.text: Lo! Ours it is (to give) the guidance

12.author: Sarwar
12.text: Surely, in Our hands is guidance,

12.author: Shakir
12.text: Surely Ours is it to show the way,

12.author: Wahiduddin Khan
12.text: Surely, it is for Us to provide guidance --

12.author: Yusuf Ali
12.text: Verily We take upon Ourselves to guide,

13.author: Ahmed Ali
13.text: And to Us belong the End and the Beginning.

13.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
13.text: And indeed the Hereafter and this world both belong to Us.

13.author: Arberry
13.text: and to Us belong the Last and the First.

13.author: Daryabadi
13.text: And verify Ours is both the Hereafter and the life present.

13.author: Hilali & Khan
13.text: And truly, unto Us (belong) the last (Hereafter) and the first (this world).

13.author: Itani
13.text: And to Us belong the Last and the First.

13.author: Maududi
13.text: and to Us belong the Next Life and the present.

13.author: Mubarakpuri
13.text: And truly, unto Us (belong) the last (Hereafter) and the first (this world).

13.author: Pickthall
13.text: And lo! unto Us belong the latter portion and the former.

13.author: Qarai
13.text: and to Us belong the world and the Hereafter.

13.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
13.text: and to Us belong the Last and the First.

13.author: Saheeh International
13.text: And indeed, to Us belongs the Hereafter and the first [life].

13.author: Sarwar
13.text: and to Us belong the hereafter and the worldly life.

13.author: Shakir
13.text: And most surely Ours is the hereafter and the former.

13.author: Wahiduddin Khan
13.text: and to Us belongs the Hereafter as well as the present world --

13.author: Yusuf Ali
13.text: And verily unto Us (belong) the End and the Beginning.

14.author: Ahmed Ali
14.text: So, I warn you of the blazing Fire.

14.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
14.text: I therefore warn you of the fire that is ablaze.

14.author: Arberry
14.text: Now I have warned you of a Fire that flames,

14.author: Daryabadi
14.text: Wherefore have warned you of Fire flaming,

14.author: Hilali & Khan
14.text: Therefore I have warned you of a Fire blazing fiercely (Hell);

14.author: Itani
14.text: I have warned you of a Fierce Blaze.

14.author: Maududi
14.text: I have now warned you of a Blazing Fire,

14.author: Mubarakpuri
14.text: Therefore I have warned you of a Fire Talazza.

14.author: Pickthall
14.text: Therefor have I warned you of the flaming Fire

14.author: Qarai
14.text: So I warn you of a blazing fire,

14.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
14.text: I have now warned you of the Blazing Fire,

14.author: Saheeh International
14.text: So I have warned you of a Fire which is blazing.

14.author: Sarwar
14.text: I have warned you about the fierce blazing fire

14.author: Shakir
14.text: Therefore I warn you of the fire that flames:

14.author: Wahiduddin Khan
14.text: I have warned you then about a raging Fire:

14.author: Yusuf Ali
14.text: Therefore do I warn you of a Fire blazing fiercely;

15.author: Ahmed Ali
15.text: No one will burn in it but the most wretched,

15.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
15.text: None except the most wicked will enter it.

15.author: Arberry
15.text: whereat none but the most wretched shall be roasted,

15.author: Daryabadi
15.text: None shall roast therein but the most wretched,

15.author: Hilali & Khan
15.text: None shall enter it save the most wretched,

15.author: Itani
15.text: None will burn in it except the very wicked.

15.author: Maududi
15.text: where none shall burn except the most wicked,

15.author: Mubarakpuri
15.text: None shall enter it save the most wretched.

15.author: Pickthall
15.text: Which only the most wretched must endure,

15.author: Qarai
15.text: which none shall enter except the most wretched [of persons]

15.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
15.text: in which none shall be roasted except the most wretched sinner,

15.author: Saheeh International
15.text: None will [enter to] burn therein except the most wretched one.

15.author: Sarwar
15.text: in which no one will suffer forever

15.author: Shakir
15.text: None shall enter it but the most unhappy,

15.author: Wahiduddin Khan
15.text: none shall enter it but the most wicked,

15.author: Yusuf Ali
15.text: None shall reach it but those most unfortunate ones

16.author: Ahmed Ali
16.text: Who denied (the truth) and turned away.

16.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
16.text: The one who denied and turned away.

16.author: Arberry
16.text: even he who cried lies, and turned away;

16.author: Itani
16.text: He who denies and turns away.

16.author: Maududi
16.text: who rejected the Truth, calling it falsehood and turned his back on it.

16.author: Pickthall
16.text: He who denieth and turneth away.

16.author: Qarai
16.text: —he who impugns [God’s prophets] and turns his back.

16.author: Saheeh International
16.text: Who had denied and turned away.

16.author: Sarwar
16.text: except the wicked ones who have rejected the (Truth) and have turned away from it.

16.author: Shakir
16.text: Who gives the lie (to the truth) and turns (his) back.

16.author: Wahiduddin Khan
16.text: who denied [the truth], and turned away.

16.author: Yusuf Ali
16.text: Who give the lie to Truth and turn their backs.

17.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
17.text: He will be far away from the most pious. (The first Caliph S. Abu Bakr Siddiq)

17.author: Arberry
17.text: and from which the most godfearing shall be removed,

17.author: Daryabadi
17.text: And avoid it shall the most pious,

17.author: Hilali & Khan
17.text: And Al-Muttaqun (the pious and righteous - see V. 2:2) will be far removed from it (Hell).

17.author: Itani
17.text: But the devout will avoid it.

17.author: Maududi
17.text: But the God-fearing shall be kept away from it,

17.author: Mubarakpuri
17.text: And those with Taqwa will be far removed from it.

17.author: Pickthall
17.text: Far removed from it will be the righteous

17.author: Qarai
17.text: The Godwary [person] will be spared of that

17.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
17.text: and from which the cautious shall be distanced.

17.author: Saheeh International
17.text: But the righteous one will avoid it -

17.author: Sarwar
17.text: The pious ones who spend for the cause of God

17.author: Shakir
17.text: And away from it shall be kept the one who guards most (against evil),

17.author: Wahiduddin Khan
17.text: One who fears God shall be kept away from it --

17.author: Yusuf Ali
17.text: But those most devoted to Allah shall be removed far from it,-

18.author: Ahmed Ali
18.text: And gives of his wealth that he may grow in virtue,

18.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
18.text: Who gives his wealth in order to be pure.

18.author: Arberry
18.text: even he who gives his wealth. to purify himself

18.author: Daryabadi
18.text: Who giveth away his substance that he may be cleansed,

18.author: Hilali & Khan
18.text: He who spends his wealth for increase in self-purification,

18.author: Itani
18.text: He who gives his money to become pure.

18.author: Maududi
18.text: the Godfearing who spends his wealth to purify himself;

18.author: Mubarakpuri
18.text: He who gives of his wealth for self-purification.

18.author: Pickthall
18.text: Who giveth his wealth that he may grow (in goodness).

18.author: Qarai
18.text: —he who gives his wealth to purify himself

18.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
18.text: He who gives his wealth to be purified,

18.author: Saheeh International
18.text: [He] who gives [from] his wealth to purify himself

18.author: Sarwar
18.text: and purify themselves will be safe from this fire.

18.author: Shakir
18.text: Who gives away his wealth, purifying himself

18.author: Wahiduddin Khan
18.text: one who gives his wealth to become purified,

18.author: Yusuf Ali
18.text: Those who spend their wealth for increase in self-purification,

19.author: Ahmed Ali
19.text: And is under no one's obligation to return his favour,

19.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
19.text: And no one has done a favour to him, for which he should be compensated.

19.author: Arberry
19.text: and confers no favour on any man for recompense,

19.author: Daryabadi
19.text: And who hath no favour from anyone to recompense.

19.author: Hilali & Khan
19.text: And have in his mind no favour from anyone for which a reward is expected in return,

19.author: Maududi
19.text: not as payment for any favours that he received,

19.author: Mubarakpuri
19.text: And who has (in mind) no favor from anyone to be paid back.

19.author: Pickthall
19.text: And none hath with him any favour for reward,

19.author: Qarai
19.text: and does not expect any reward from anyone,

19.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
19.text: and confers no favor upon anyone for recompense

19.author: Saheeh International
19.text: And not [giving] for anyone who has [done him] a favor to be rewarded

19.author: Sarwar
19.text: They do not expect any reward

19.author: Shakir
19.text: And no one has with him any boon for which he should be rewarded,

19.author: Wahiduddin Khan
19.text: and owes no favour to anyone, which is to be repaid,

19.author: Yusuf Ali
19.text: And have in their minds no favour from anyone for which a reward is expected in return,

19.author: Transliteration
19.text: Wama liahadin AAindahu min niAAmatin tujza

20.author: Ahmed Ali
20.text: Other than seeking the glory of his Lord, most high,

20.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
20.text: He desires only to please his Lord, the Supreme.

20.author: Arberry
20.text: only seeking the Face of his Lord the Most High;

20.author: Daryabadi
20.text: But only seeking the Countenance of his Lord the Most High.

20.author: Hilali & Khan
20.text: Except only the desire to seek the Countenance of his Lord, the Most High;

20.author: Itani
20.text: Only seeking the acceptance of his Lord, the Most High.

20.author: Maududi
20.text: but only to seek the good pleasure of his Lord Most High.

20.author: Mubarakpuri
20.text: Except to seek the Face of his Lord, the Most High.

20.author: Pickthall
20.text: Except as seeking (to fulfil) the purpose of his Lord Most High.

20.author: Qarai
20.text: but seek only the pleasure of their Lord, the Most Exalted,

20.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
20.text: seeking only the Face of his Lord, the Most High,

20.author: Saheeh International
20.text: But only seeking the countenance of his Lord, Most High.

20.author: Sarwar
20.text: except the pleasure of their Lord, the Most High

20.author: Shakir
20.text: Except the seeking of the pleasure of his Lord, the Most High.

20.author: Wahiduddin Khan
20.text: acting only for the sake of his Lord the Most High --

20.author: Yusuf Ali
20.text: But only the desire to seek for the Countenance of their Lord Most High;

21.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
21.text: And indeed, soon he will be very pleased.

21.author: Arberry
21.text: and he shall surely be satisfied.

21.author: Daryabadi
21.text: And presently he shall become wellpleased.

21.author: Hilali & Khan
21.text: He surely will be pleased (when he will enter Paradise).

21.author: Maududi
21.text: He will surely be well-pleased (with him).

21.author: Qarai
21.text: and, surely, soon they will be well-pleased.

21.author: Saheeh International
21.text: And he is going to be satisfied.

21.author: Sarwar
21.text: and the reward (of their Lord) will certainly make them happy.

21.author: Shakir
21.text: And he shall soon be well-pleased.

21.author: Wahiduddin Khan
21.text: and before long he will be well satisfied.

21.author: Yusuf Ali
21.text: And soon will they attain (complete) satisfaction.
